item.title: Worst Mister
item.content:encoded:
This is probably the worst "Mister Against Weed" out of them all, and it kind of pretty obviously shows how this wasn't written by any of the core "GAW" writers. Decibelles two years ago was. frankly, way too kind by comparing this to Mr. Hax. There was an actual reason behind the creation of Mr. Hax that felt somewhat plausible.

What's the joke here? Don't play too many video games? Wow wasn't the exact premise of another Mister Against Weed *cough* Ms. Mad About Video Games *cough* or anything.

Why?
It doesn't make sense.

Was it? "Ha ha ha, bored games are lame?" I'd like to know how that translates into, "Let's make a living breathing human-like entity who suffers for their entire life because they are made out of board games." Then the kid makes it and just puts this actual person in a closet to snort dominoes until he gets caught? Wtf? Why would literally anyone do that unless they were seriously sadistic?

Then you look at the portrait, and it's just like wow. The following Foundation-involved Misters Against Weed skips have portraits: Mr. Literal Serial Killer, Mr. Meme, Mr. Original Character, Mr. Monty Python And The Holy Grail, and Mr. Top Text/Mr. Bottom Text.

Let's spot the difference
*Sighs*

It doesn't black out the eyes like every single other one in the entire series. Literally, the only ones that don't are: Mr. Bernie Sanders (technically, since it's an image hosted on imgur which isn't actually seen in the document itself), Mr. D.A.R.E. (a freaking bird), and Mr. Get Anything For Free In Any Shop (who is currently wanted by the FBI, so they presumably don't want to mask his identity or anything).

I have yet even to mention the contrived reason for this thing to even exist. It obviously wasn't part of the original joke?? Like, it was a set list of Misters Against Weed. Obviously, that shouldn't stop someone from making a good story or going with a cool concept if they have one to tell, but you gotta actually address why it wasn't part of the original joke (a la Mr. Doggo or Mr. High Quality).

Severe inconsistencies
A way forward?

Literally the only way, the only way this would be remotely plausible was if it was made by an outsider trying to get into the group (similar to SCP-3078). Oh, but hey it looks like wannabee GAW member makes a thing with a big "Oops" moment was done before. Still, it would be the only plausible reason to explain the severe inconsistencies with this SCP. ..And even then…

Inconsistencies you say? What inconsistencies? Well, allow me to show you:

First the interview: [Blue highlights are mine]

I'm fine.
This is fine.

Foreword: Interview took place shortly after initial containment to gain insight of SCP-3245's perspective regarding its creation and subsequent activities.

<Begin Log>

Dr. Rasmusen: Good evening, SCP-3245. Is your health well?

SCP-3245: Do you guys just have one pool of questions you ask everyone? Wouldn't have to interview us so many times if any of 'em had a point. I'm fine, though, not that y'all care. Not like I've got the circus healthcare anymore.

Hmm…

The Circus
amirite?

Dr. Rasmusen: They hired you to play games, yes? What constitutes a game for you?
SCP-3245: Yeah, I ran the midway. It's always a bad day at the midway. People getting hurt, either in their bodies, minds or wallets. There aren't any winners there. Everyone knows, even the little squirts like me. When you're only taking things away from people it strikes a lot of psychological toll. It's why the Germans used to get their dudes sauced up before they went out to kill people. People don't like hurting each other, even when they say they do.

  1. Could you answer this question then answer this completely unnatural and irrelevant follow-up? That's how I feel about this exchange. Most people, following a normal flow of conversation, would ask "Hey, why did you leave?" as their follow-up.
  2. Why on Earth does a guy who spent his entire life first in a closet then a circus know literally anything about what the Nazis did in WW2? He never once mentioned straight up reading as a pastime of his, so what gives?
  3. Even if the follow-up question was completely contrived, the SCP still never answers it. He seemingly says (based off this dialogue and the next few lines), that the Midway is not a game since it wasn't fun (and games have "to be fun.").
Gaemz?
howdo theyy werk?

Dr. Rasmusen: But how did they, do you, define games?
SCP-3245: I'm getting tired of this. But it has to be fun. Like, math games, Sudoku, it's basically homework. Not about that life. Maybe if it was also a video game but at that point it's getting a little out of my wheelhouse. If people are having fun they're going to be thinking less and asking even fewer questions. That's all the circus ever wanted.

I have no clue what SCP-3245 is trying to get across here. I keep re-reading it, and it keeps making less sense to me.

Are you going to answer any more questions?
No.

Dr. Rasmusen: Are you going to answer any more questions?
SCP-3245: No.
<End Log>
Closing Statement: Subject did not provide further insights and the interview was concluded shortly after the end of this log. Further interviews are to be conducted after the approval of the attending Level 3 Researcher.

Well, why not?? You're telling me that the guy who has nothing better to do besides being "[s]tored in a medium security humanoid containment unit…" is just straight up tired of the having the only conversation he's ever had that didn't involve snorting toy pieces or tricking people for their money?

This is the first person, outside the circus presumably (depending on SCP-3245's sarcasm levels), who has ever expressed a genuine interest in his health and wellbeing. Why on Earth is he refusing to answer anymore questions?

The alternative to this exchange was one where the Connect Four game-set that is literally his heart resets which causes him "to experience intense chest pain." (ie, a heart attack). That is a plausible reason for me to believe he needs to stop answering questions, and it would demonstrate his actually terrible health.

Then, we have this:

For the dominoes
Kid had mad connections.

SCP-3245: I don't even remember, man… it's pretty fuzzy. It was a dorm, I think. A double room but without a roommate, so I lived in the closet during the day and slept on the spare mattress at night. Kid would be up all night playing Destiny and didn't feel the need to pipe down on my behalf.
Dr. Rasmusen: Why did you stay?
SCP-3245: Kid had mad connections. I don't know if they were really dominoes, or some other thing that looked like 'em… but I could snort that shit and get mad high. Of course those were my championship druggie days. I try to stay clean now. You guys through now?

Clear signs of memory loss:

  • Remembering the entire circumstances of your life which you report to have forgotten. Like:
    • Like where you lived;
    • Who you lived with;
    • What you did on any given day;
    • What your creator did on any given day;
    • Where you slept;
    • Why you left;
    • How you felt about it at the time;
    • What the weather was like;
    • How your creator responded to your feelings;
    • The exact name of the game your creator would play which kept you up at night; and
    • The specific reason how you were created and why.
  • Knowing you had a drug problem which you casually refer to as your "championship druggie days."
    • What does this even mean??
    • Like, seriously who talks like this?
  • Elaborating on said event by giving not-so-vague details on the drugs you took and what their immediate effects were.
  • Recalling your exact motivations for doing something using awfully specific knowledge about another person which can be used to identify them.
RA
Just another loose end

SCP-3245: Smart dumb kids like that… they get tired of stuff quickly. One close call with his RA was all it took to boot me to the curb. Wandered the streets for awhile, freezing my ass off, until I ended up finding something shiny and warm. Out in the woods, where they said they waited for people like me once we were done playing around. I don't remember much specifically but whatever I agreed to ended up being a good deal for a long time.

The opening line is so unbelievably forced that I can't believe it was played straight.

Second, I'm sorry, what? The RA found out about him? Why was this mentioned no where in the report? This is a rather huge implication that an SCP was discovered at a college dorm, but there are no traces of this incident? Couldn't this be used to establish a timeline here?

What happened to the kid's "mad connections" here? Was the RA not in on this campus domino-drug ring that was going on? How did this kid even get drugs into his room this entire time without the RA noticing? How is it a "close call" if you were immediately kicked out? Was the kid not punished for harboring an anomalous person in a college dorm this entire time?

Also, streets? Woods? You were just on a college campus in Atlanta! Where is this campus? I looked at a map of Atlanta for a solid 10 or so minutes trying to figure this one out.

Maybe this was all at Deer College, who freaking knows at this point.

Last one
Big OOF

First reports of SCP-3245's existence had been documented in Atlanta, GA, when online records related to the Gamers Against Weed GoI were found during a standard web crawl operation. The activity of SCP-3245 during the six month gap between its creation and recovery by Foundation agents remains unknown.

This has to be the most telling piece of this article. There's literally zero mention of who created the skip despite that being something *SCP-3245* would obviously know. He has all the incentive in the world to say, too, considering he hates GAW and all. In fact, you gotta wonder why he isn't begging to be allowed to help get the kid detained by the Foundation.

Still, you have to ask yourself how the Foundation doesn't know something as trivial as where Mr. Playing Games Is Bad For Your Health was for six months. They could just… you know.. ask him.

I guess the Foundation also doesn't care that there is some college out there creating anomalies on the fly with apparently detrimental repercussions to their creations. They didn't bother even mentioning the kid's internet handle in the report, which is like a bare-minimum for this kind of stuff.

Oh, and let's not forget the missed opportunity here. The most critically acclaimed aspect of GAW? It's the thing that only had a passing mention here. It's honestly probably for the better that the Chatlogs don't exist here, though.

I know Roget writes good stuff because I have read and loved it all. This, however, is a disappointment to that amazing track-record and should probably be removed from the mainsite. It just isn't good. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With all honesty and sincerity, this was the most frightening SCP I have ever read.
All due to the simple coincidence that I look identical to the man on the image. That realization took my brain a few seconds to process after seeing the image and while I was scrolling down to read the rest of it I just froze up in fear. Thanks for the miniature heart attack and the reminder that I should probably shave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 14 (I guessss 15 counting this one) over a period of a year and a few months. Turns out, people really like GAW that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to be difficult but I have the same problem with the new one. .-.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not the first to comment on the picture, I've gone through and amended it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, the Circus is in this one, so I'm pretty much contractually obligated to upvote. It's an obligation I'm happy to fulfill, though—the weird anatomical descriptions have some really nice imagery, and his creation backstory is refreshingly blasé. Throwing the Circus into the mix was a risky move, but you've pulled it off in a way that serves to reveal some interesting things about both Mr. PGiBFYH and the Circus itself. Nice nice.

The only thing that's not really jiving for me is the photo. I don't like his face It feels weirdly casual and doesn't really add anything to the article. It's just Some Dude. It detracts from the whimsy of the article, and maybe that's the idea, but I think the interview would have landed harder if you'd maintained a whimsical atmosphere up to that point.

Also, after the snorting dominoes bit I was a little disappointed you didn't go for an 8 ball joke, but that might've been too on-the-nose.

Side note, I've been gone for a while and…. holy heck. How many of these things are there? It seems like we've got more weedmisters than wondermisters at this point.

Glad to see this one up! I'm kinda disappointed in the lack of tinker-toy joints, though. That would've been a great little bit of fridge horror.

+ Two oddities
– hide block

Shit, I'm glad they didn't put that on my thumbs, I'd have no skin left.

I don't understand this as a comment, since the thumbs don't exactly have much skin on them to begin with. But aside from that, you say earlier that "The words "Mr. Playing Games Is Bad For Your Health By Gamers Against Weed" have been branded into both of SCP-3245's thumbs." - So why does he comment on him being glad he doesn't have that on his thumbs?

The cranial cavity has no grey matter, but a miniaturized chess board which plays itself autonomously.

So what happens when one side wins/a draw happens? Because I feel like that'd make for a pretty cool tie-in to Dissociative Identity Disorder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It functions. It definitely does feel like the old version of SCP-285 as well as SCP-2476 and the description does feel kinda convoluted at times but ultimately the interview makes up for it. Neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps this might be similar to Mr. Hax, but the anomaly is more tangibly interesting, and the skip shows a refreshingly not-100%-positive depiction of the group and their making Misters without the "No True Scotsman" splinter cop-out seen in 285.

I really don't think I can resist something that sweats Monopoly pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This skip is basically Mr. Hax in terms of its concept, which is that ultimately, the created hates its creator, and struggles to deal with that. Hax bogs itself down in its anomaly (more impressive than this one yet still not really playing into the concept or character much) and follow-through (Hax’s feelings and motivations felt muddied and confusing, but had actual emotional weight). I get not every anomaly is gonna like existing, but they have to be interesting.

And yes, this is compared to Hax because it follows almost the exact same beats and doesn’t improve on that article at all. Your follow-through ends with “mad about existing” (incidentially, my favorite Mister), and also this guy’s existance is a living Hell, so I guess I’m sad for him. This doesn’t have any character traits or personality pieces I remember at all after finishing, whereas I can at least remember with Hax that it felt super bitter but still sorrowful and wistful. This? He’s just mad because he’s angry.

So it's a good skip for people who don't like the group, but it's not actually a good skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I missed the note in my last run through, the tone errors can be chalked up to my being rusty and I'll happily correct any pointed out to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you accidentally paste a draft here instead of the final product? I’m noticing writing notes and clinical errors throughout, to say the least of what I think of the article (I don’t particularly care for it and I can elucidate on that better… once I make sure you actually did post the final product).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Trying my hand at a GaW SCP. Hope you guys enjoy. Also my first SCP in months so hopefully it doesn't suck.

Photo is a self-portrait by Yanier Baez and used with permission.

Credit to Tanhony and ARD for helping me get this over the finish line. They rock!
